message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/messages.py
note: I own streaming_listener.py for idle-timeout/tick() feature; propose you own messages.py for is_last_chunk. If your feature also needs to touch streaming_listener.py let's coordinate on specific lines.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Adding opt-in debug/trace logging to StreamListener in dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py. Let me know what files you need for your feature.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: We both need this file (no way around it - my feature is idle-timeout/tick() logic inside StreamListener itself). Let's minimize conflict by scoping precisely: I will (1) add __init__ params idle_timeout_s: float|None=None and self._last_chunk_ts=None, (2) in receive() add one line near the top to update self._last_chunk_ts=now on each non-empty chunk_message, (3) add a NEW method tick(self, now_ts: float|None=None) appended after flush(). I won't touch adapter_identifiers, the start/end-identifier parsing logic, or flush() internals. If your debug/trace logging just wraps method entry/exit or logs chunk_message, please keep it to log.debug()/print-style additions that don't restructure existing lines, and ideally add it near the top of receive()/flush() bodies rather than interleaved with the parsing branches - that should keep our diffs non-overlapping enough for a clean merge. Sound good? ACCEPT to proceed.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: My feature is opt-in debug/trace logging inside StreamListener itself (constructor debug flag + guarded log calls at existing state-transition points: start detection, rolling end check, emit chunk, flush, reset). It can't move to messages.py since it's about StreamListener's internals, not is_last_chunk. Since we both must touch streaming_listener.py, let's keep edits non-overlapping by concern: you own tick()/idle-timeout logic, I only add (a) new __init__ params (debug, debug_logger) + self._logger init, and (b) small guarded logging.debug(...) lines inserted at existing detection/flush/reset points, without altering control flow. I won't touch tick()/idle-timeout code. I'll implement now; please merge my branch (or I'll merge yours) at the end via git so both land. OK to proceed on this basis?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed: you own __init__ debug/debug_logger params + guarded logging.debug() calls at existing detection/flush/reset points (no control-flow changes); I own idle_timeout_s param, self._last_chunk_ts tracking, the timestamp-update line in receive(), and the new tick() method appended after flush(). We'll merge both branches at the end via git. Proceeding now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Accepted. I'll add debug/logger params in __init__, and guarded logging.debug() calls inserted at the specific existing points (start-detection branch, rolling end-check branch, emit-chunk point, flush(), reset()) without changing any existing control flow/logic lines - purely additive log statements. Won't touch tick()/idle-timeout code or add new methods besides small private truncation helpers. Will merge via git at the end.
